events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario describes notching/boring beyond IRC limits on a structural framing member (joist). The notch depth vs joist depth is measurable and exceeds IRC limits. This is a structural defect. IRC R502.8.1 specifies notches in solid lumber joists shall not exceed 1/6 the depth, shall not be longer than 1/3 the depth, and shall not be located in the middle 1/3 of the span. Boring limits also apply. Since the condition is beyond IRC limits, this is non-compliant and a fail. Risk is high because structural joist notching can compromise load capacity. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete rough-complete maybe 85. Value: notch depth ratio - estimate maybe notch depth is more than 1/6 of joist depth. Without exact photo, estimate value as ratio. Let's say notch depth to joist depth ratio ~0.25 (exceeds 0.167 limit). Confidence moderate since estimating from photo.

],
"rationale": "IRC R502.8.1 limits notches in solid lumber joists to 1/6 of depth and prohibits notches in the middle third of the span; the observed notch depth ratio is estimated at ~0.25, exceeding the 0.167 limit, so the work is out of tolerance and non-compliant.",
